questions.question: The Dutch East India Company established its first factory in India in _________.
In which of the following years were the Montagu–Chelmsford Reforms introduced by the British Government in India to gradually establish self-governing institutions?
What key emotion did the Extremists aim to arouse in people against British colonial rule?
Which of the following is NOT a major cause of the people’s revolts before 1857?
questions.option_2: Exploitation of rural society by corrupt officers.

questions.question: Joseph Francois Dupleix was the governor who led the forces of which of the following European powers?
What does ‘dyarchy’ mean in the context of the 1919 Act?
questions.option_3: Power to the judiciary and legislature

questions.question: India’s foreign trade structure changed significantly under British rule. During the colonial period, what kind of goods did India primarily export?
Which of the following is an example of social legislation introduced by the British during the colonial period in India?
Which of the following European powers was the first to arrive in India by sea route?
In which of the following regions did Rani Avantibai Lodhi of Ramgarh raise and lead an army of four thousand against the British, who had taken over the administration of her state?
Who was the last Viceroy of India who oversaw the partition of India before leaving?
Which of the following was one of the last princely states to join the Indian Union?
Who was declared the Emperor of India by the revolting sepoys during the 1857 uprising?
What was the name of the legend who spearheaded the Munda Rebellion?
In which of the following years was Indian Universities Act enacted to impose stiff control over Indian Universities?
The Faraizi Movement's no-tax campaign primarily targeted the ________.
In which of the following years did Sri Aurobindo resign from his post in Baroda and move to Calcutta, where he soon became one of the leaders of the Nationalist movement?
Who among the following was proclaimed as the leader of the revolt by the rebels during the First War of Independence, 1857, against British oppression?
The Regulating Act of 1773 stipulated that a Supreme Court be established in Calcutta with one Chief Justice and _________.
Which region witnessed the strongest violence and martial law enforcement during the Rowlatt Satyagraha?
Which harmful social practice was banned in 1829 by Lord Bentinck during the British rule in India?
The 1962 Sino-Indian War was primarily fought over which geographical region?
The opening of which of the following canals intensified British control over India’s foreign trade?
The drain of wealth theory was a major critique of colonial economic policies. Who propounded the ‘Drain of wealth theory’ in the late 19ᵗʰ century?
Which event is considered the turning point in Gandhi’s political life, converting him from a moderate to a mass leader?
